reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real inference verified end-to-end) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real q8 WASM inference verified end-to-end on a no-GPU runner via ?auto: main page reported 'Labelled 722000 pixels across 13 classes', 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests. Standalone probe on the portrait: Upper-clothes 13.5% (dominant garment, correct), plus Hair/Face/Pants/shoes/limbs — a grounded human/clothing parse. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/segformer-b2-clothes/desktop.png, mobile.png
rubric.notes: SegFormer-B2 image-segmentation pipeline (Xenova/segformer_b2_clothes), inference in models/segformer-b2-clothes/worker.js.

rubric.evidence: Output is the documented [{label, mask:RawImage}, …] per-class list; folded to an 18-class argmax label map. Grounded: on the cardigan portrait Upper-clothes dominates (13.5%); practical page detected 3 garment classes; multi-model CLIP ranked the 1940s portrait 'vintage clothing 24.6%' (top). Latency ~2.7 s q8 WASM.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity confirmed against real output on three rights-safe sample images.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares model id (Xenova/segformer_b2_clothes, linked), task (image-segmentation), backend (WebAssembly), quantisation (q8), architecture (SegFormer MiT-B2), and honest license ('Other — see model card'). Inference off the main thread (new Worker). conformance declares-* assertions derived from this metadata.

Loads through shared createModelLoader auto-init; verified Download→downloading→ready state machine on a fresh profile (loader status 'Downloading model… N%' → 'Model ready — running locally'). Per-model clear-cache control present.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced; verified the fresh-profile Download path directly.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live region announces checking/downloading/ready/error; errors surface on the page (status .err); load failure path exposes Retry via the loader. WASM path needs no WebGPU (runs on the no-GPU runner).
rubric.notes: honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback are manual-evidenced; download-progress + WASM-run verified.

rubric.evidence: Every control verified: sample thumbs (click + Enter/Space), drop/upload, Parse button, opacity slider, garment-extract chips (practical), garment + colour + strength (wild), style-labels textarea + two loaders (multi-model). All keyboard-operable, labelled, ≥44px tap targets in CLOTHES_CSS.

All visible output (garment map, coverage bars, tags, cut-out, recolour, CLIP style scores) is produced by the on-device runs; no canned results. Verified across basics/practical/wild/multi-model via ?auto.

All content images carry alt text; all buttons have accessible names (conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names pass); canvases have aria-labels; live regions announce state; visible focus outlines.

responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Measured document overflow 0px at desktop (1280×800) AND mobile (360×740, DPR3) on the main page + all four use-case pages, after fixing the at-a-glance table (table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere). pre blocks scroll internally (overflowX:auto).
rubric.notes: Both classes flipped to ok on real agent evidence + screenshots.

rubric.evidence: Inference + the dense per-pixel argmax→colour composite run in the worker; the overlay ImageBitmap is built via OffscreenCanvas.transferToImageBitmap and TRANSFERRED, so the main thread only drawImage()s it. Opacity drags re-drawImage the cached bitmap (no putImageData).
rubric.notes: inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced; off-thread composite verified in worker.js.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy work off the main thread; yield/transfer instead of blocking.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — SegFormer inference AND the per-pixel argmax→colour composite run in models/segformer-b2-clothes/worker.js; the worker builds the overlay ImageBitmap via OffscreenCanvas.transferToImageBitmap and TRANSFERS it, so the main thread only drawImage()s it (invariant 15). conformance inference-off-main-thread passes.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive image canvas overlay without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid, contained layout; intrinsic sizing; never let content force page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — stage/viz canvases use inline-size:100%; block-size:auto; max-block-size; object-fit:contain; the at-a-glance table uses table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere so long HF ids/URLs wrap instead of forcing width. Fixed a measured 59px mobile overflow this way. responsive-check: desktop clean, mobile clean (0px).

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress and status live region, keyboard controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: role=status aria-live for async state; labelled keyboard-operable controls; alt on images; ≥44px targets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader + page status use role=status aria-live=polite; sample thumbs and chips are keyboard-operable (Enter/Space) with visible focus; all images have alt; swatch/chip buttons are min-block-size:44px. conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names pass.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6, shared design system).

openQuestions: Does Xenova/segformer_b2_clothes return a real 18-class human-parsing label map at q8 on WASM? (agent-verified: yes — Upper-clothes/Pants/Hair/Face/shoes/limbs with correct non-zero pixel counts on the portrait; Upper-clothes dominant.)
Does the multi-model outfit→CLIP composition ground correctly? (agent-verified: yes — the 1940s studio portrait ranks 'vintage clothing' top at 24.6%.)

followUpGoals.goal: Add a body-part vs garment split view and a crowd/multi-person coverage breakdown for the market/street samples.

summary: Revision-1 agent matrix pass for segformer-b2-clothes (image-segmentation / human + clothing parsing, Xenova/segformer_b2_clothes, q8 WASM). Real in-browser inference verified end-to-end on a no-GPU runner (722k px, 18 classes, Upper-clothes dominant on the portrait), all four use-case pages produce real grounded output (fashion tags, garment cut-out, outfit recolour, outfit→CLIP style), mobile+desktop responsive matrix clean (0px overflow both classes, light+dark), and the dense composite runs off-thread. Distinct capability: dense human/clothing parsing, not covered by the built matting/face-parsing/background-removal demos. All auto-checkable conformance assertions pass; manual dimensions agent-verdicted from real e2e output.
